description: The stable surface external tools (BuddhistLLM, ocr_pipeline) read for edition identity. Normative prose: docs/access/external_tool_dependency_contract.md. This descriptor is language-neutral on purpose: a consumer verifies against it WITHOUT importing catalogue code. The same version is stamped into every DB as schema_meta.external_read_contract_version.

v_holding_files.purpose: Per-holding file rows carrying edition identity + version, for external handoff. Excludes holdings with no file_path.

columns.edition_id: internal catalogue edition integer — NOT a stable external key; use pub_id
columns.pub_id: stable opaque UUID — the external identity key (S1: write-once, never rebinds)
columns.file_path: current catalogue path of the holding file (look up live; never store as a key)
columns.content_hash: version token for derived-artifact staleness (never a join key)
columns.provenance_kind: how the holding's text was produced

resolve.purpose: Follow a stored pub_id to its canonical live edition (S2 forwarding). A consumer that resolves is robust to merges/withdrawals; a producer may snapshot pub_id and defer resolution to the consumer.

columns.superseded_by: forwarding pointer to the winning edition id on merge/withdraw; NULL when this row is canonical
columns.title: live display title

guarantees.S1: A pub_id is write-once and never resolves to an unrelated edition (no rebind, no id reuse).
guarantees.S2: A pub_id always resolves; merges/withdrawals leave a forwarding pointer via edition.superseded_by.
guarantees.S3: Consumer discipline: store only pub_id (identity) + content_hash (version); look everything else up live by pub_id.

handshake.how: A consumer reads schema_meta.external_read_contract_version from the DB it already connects to, confirms it is >= the version it was built for, and (tolerant reader) confirms the columns it needs are present via PRAGMA table_info. No shared code, no extra file.
handshake.compatibility: The read surface grows ADDITIVELY within a major version: new columns may appear, so consumers must select named columns and ignore extras. Removing/renaming a column or weakening a guarantee bumps this version.
